name: Do You Know Who I Am?

description.value:
Frequency once per hour

Your reputation casts a long shadow, allowing you to throw your cultural weight around, especially in larger settlements. With a well-timed tirade or attention-getting shout, you can break a foe's mind and bring them low. Choose a foe within 30 feet, and attempt an @Check[intimidation|against:will] check against the target's Will DC. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to this check if you're in a city or a +2 circumstance bonus to this check if you're in a metropolis. If you're specifically in Absalom, the bonus is instead +3. The target is then temporarily immune to your Do You Know Who I Am? for 24 hours.

Critical Success The target takes @Damage[12d6[mental]] damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 3}. It can't reduce its frightened condition below 1 if it ends its turn adjacent to you.

Success The target takes @Damage[6d6[mental]] damage and becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened]{Frightened 2}.

Failure The target is unaffected.

Critical Failure The target is unaffected, and the embarrassment causes you to take @Damage[6d6[mental]] damage.
